title: Cook Rice on the Stove
summary: Learn the right ratio of rice to water and how to simmer it so it comes out fluffy and tender.
learnerPromise: Cook rice that is fluffy, not mushy or crunchy.
whyItMatters: Rice is a cheap, filling base for many meals. Knowing how to cook it well means you can make a satisfying dinner with just a few ingredients.
realLifeUses: Make rice as a side dish for dinner
Prepare rice bowls with toppings you already have
Cook a big batch to use for several meals
Replace instant rice with better-tasting home-cooked rice
youWillLearn: How much water to use for different types of rice
How to bring rice to a boil and then simmer it
How to tell when rice is done cooking
miniChallenge: Measure rice and water, bring to a boil, then simmer until tender.
steps: Measure 1 cup of white rice and rinse it under cold water until the water runs mostly clear.
Put the rinsed rice in a pot with 2 cups of water and a small pinch of salt.
Place the pot on the stove, turn the heat to high, and bring it to a full boil.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot with a lid, and set a timer for 15 minutes.
When the timer goes off, remove the pot from the heat and let it sit, still covered, for 5 minutes before fluffing with a fork.
completionCriteria: Rice is tender and most of the water is absorbed
Rice is not burnt on the bottom of the pot
commonProblems: Rice is too crunchy — it likely needs a few more minutes of simmering with the lid on
Rice is mushy — too much water was used; try slightly less next time
Rice stuck to the bottom — the heat may have been too high after boiling
tips: Do not lift the lid during the 15-minute simmer; steam escapes and the rice dries out
Use a pot with a tight-fitting lid to trap steam
Brown rice needs more water and a longer cooking time than white rice
helpBoundary: Ask for help if you are unsure how to use the stove. Turn pot handles inward so they do not get bumped.
